references.name: WHATWG HTML Living Standard - Server-sent events
references.usedFor: Modeled reconnecting, closed subscription cleanup, UTF-8 event stream framing, and Last-Event-ID style replay as channel metadata instead of raw browser joins.
references.borrowed: EventSource has CONNECTING, OPEN, and CLOSED states, reconnect behavior, and a close method that aborts fetch activity.
references.rejected: The browser does not subscribe directly to raw multi-domain event data; the local algorithm requires safe projection contracts.

references.name: Playwright Network
references.usedFor: Added network-failure listeners and route-scoped assertions in the browser helper to prove no failed live-channel requests or raw payload network leakage.
references.borrowed: Network routing and request observation are appropriate for asserting mocked or absent browser event joins.
references.rejected: No production endpoint mocking is used to make the UI appear live; fixtures remain deterministic domain projections.

references.name: Playwright Trace Viewer
references.usedFor: Kept screenshot and trace-ready artifact output under `.artifacts/live-projection-gateway-464` for failure investigation.
references.borrowed: Trace capture is valuable for CI failure debugging.
references.rejected: Trace files are not required as committed artifacts for the deterministic local suite.

references.name: Playwright Screenshots
references.usedFor: Browser suites capture full-page evidence for normal update, stale/quarantine, and cross-surface drift states.
references.borrowed: Full-page screenshots are suitable for proving complete route posture.
references.rejected: Pixel-perfect snapshot approval is not used as the primary authority; contract tests remain authoritative.

references.name: Playwright ARIA snapshots
references.usedFor: The helper writes accessibility snapshots for the live projection strip and return-token states.
references.borrowed: ARIA snapshots help verify page structure and live status semantics.
references.rejected: Large brittle snapshots are avoided; tests assert key names and consequences.

references.name: NHS digital service manual - WCAG 2.2 changes
references.usedFor: Preserved entered context and visible focus/target behavior while live updates occur.
references.borrowed: Guidance on not hiding focused content and preserving entered data under WCAG 2.2 updates.
references.rejected: NHS components were not imported because the repository already has shell-specific patterns.

references.name: GOV.UK Design System - Notification banner
references.usedFor: Kept changed-because messages inside the relevant content instead of overusing global banners.
references.borrowed: Notification banners should be used sparingly and directly relevant content should remain in the main page.
references.rejected: No separate notification banner component was added.

references.name: GOV.UK Design System - Error summary
references.usedFor: Kept failure state copy concise and field/action-specific in adjacent panels.
references.borrowed: Validation and blocking messages should be clear, focusable, and consistent with inline messages.
references.rejected: The live gateway is not a form, so no new error-summary block is rendered.

references.name: WCAG 2.2
references.usedFor: Maintained visible focus and non-obscured selected rows during live patch and delta-gate interactions.
references.borrowed: Focus indicators should remain visible and non-obscured; pointer interactions need keyboard equivalents.
references.rejected: No custom grid keyboard model was introduced.

references.name: Government Analysis Function - Data visualisation charts
references.usedFor: Kept the live source readiness table as the parity fallback and avoided chart reshuffles for live changes.
references.borrowed: Dense visualizations should avoid clutter and preserve clear comparison.
references.rejected: No chart was added for live gateway state because a table is clearer for ten surfaces.
